hook.text: Soil contains billions of viruses per gram—more than any other habitat on the planet.

buildup.text: Most soil viruses are bacteriophages that infect and kill bacteria.

discovery.text: By killing bacteria, phages release nutrients and shift microbial community composition.

twist.text: Phages also transfer genes between bacteria, accelerating evolution in real time.

climax.text: Soil virology is a new frontier—almost nothing is known about most soil virus species.

punchline.text: The most abundant thing in soil is invisible.

quiz.question: What do most soil viruses infect?
